thinking.text:
Now I have 951 frames at 5fps. I need to extract the commands properly. Let me write a better script that:
1. For each frame, extract ALL lines that start with '>' (completed commands in history)
2. Track unique commands as they appear
3. Also detect the command being typed at the bottom (last line with '>')

But the problem is that the screen shows the command history, so each frame shows ALL previous commands. I need to identify the NEW command that appears in each frame.

Let me think about this differently. The video shows a text adventure game. Each time the player types a command and presses Enter, the game responds with new text. The screen scrolls. So I could:
1. OCR each frame to get all text
2. Compare consecutive frames to find new text
3. The new text after '>' at the bottom would be the command

But actually, a simpler approach: each frame shows the game screen. The latest command is always on the last line with '>'. If I track the last '>' line across frames, when it disappears and a new one appears, that's a completed command.

Actually, let me look at the frames more carefully. The prompt '>' appears for each command the player typed. So if I extract ALL lines starting with '>' from the FINAL frame (the last frame), I would get ALL commands that were ever typed, in order.

Let me check the last frame first.
